title: VMware NSX Distributed Firewall Micro-Segmentation Enforcement Posture — NSGroup Membership, Applied-To Scope, and Policy Coverage Gaps
exclusions: Does not replace **UC-18.2.1** volume trending of **DFW** rule hits and east-west **ALLOW**/**DROP** ratios—that UC remains the traffic-economics lens. Does not assess **Gateway Firewall** north-south policy on **Edge** nodes, **NSX Intelligence** ML planning quality in isolation, **Advanced Threat Prevention** IDS events, third-party **NFV** firewalls inside **vSphere**, or **Kubernetes Antrea** policy imported separately from **NSX** Policy domains. Does not prove regulatory **PCI** scoping alone—pair with asset inventories and file-integrity controls.
dataSources: `index=vmware` / `index=vmware_nsx` / `index=network_sec` governed naming combining **`vmware:nsxt:dfw_groups`** JSON from **`GET /policy/api/v1/infra/domains/default/groups`** (membership **`expression`**, static **VM** references, **Segment** / **LogicalPort** paths) ingested through **`Splunk_TA_vmware-nsxt`** ([Splunkbase 4856](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4856)) or companion scripted collectors, **`vmware:nsxt:dfw_policy`** snapshots of **`security-policies`** / **`rules`** including **`applied_to`**, **`source_groups`**, **`destination_groups`**, **`sequence_number`**, and **`category`**, paired **`vmware:nsxt:firewall`** plus optional **`vmware:nsxt:idfw`** verdict lines referencing **`RULE_TAG`** tuples, **`vmware:nsxt:syslog`** **Manager** alarms for collector health cross-checks, and **`vmware:vmware:vsphere:vcenter`** from **`Splunk_TA_vmware`** ([Splunkbase 1810](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1810)) so IP-only flows gain **VM** display context when **NSGroup** dynamic criteria lag **vMotion**.
app: **`Splunk_TA_vmware-nsxt`** ([Splunkbase 4856](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4856)) for **`vmware:nsxt:firewall`**, **`vmware:nsxt:idfw`**, and scripted **`vmware:nsxt:dfw_groups`** / **`vmware:nsxt:dfw_policy`** JSON landings; **`Splunk_TA_vmware`** ([Splunkbase 1810](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1810)) when **`vmware:vmware:vsphere:vcenter`** joins are required for label hygiene.
spl:
`comment("UC-18.2.2 VMware NSX micro-segmentation enforcement posture — NSGroup coverage vs DFW verdicts. Tunables earliest=-24h@h span=1h")`
(index=vmware OR index=vmware_nsx OR index=network_sec) earliest=-24h@h latest=now (sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:firewall" OR sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:idfw")
| eval verdict=upper(trim(coalesce(dfw_action,action,firewall_action,match_action,"")))
| eval verdict_norm=case(
    match(verdict,"^(ALLOW|ACCEPT)$"),"ALLOW",
    match(verdict,"^(DROP|DENY)$"),"DROP",
    match(verdict,"^(REJECT|RESET)$"),"REJECT",
    match(_raw,"(?i)\\bALLOW\\b"),"ALLOW",
    match(_raw,"(?i)\\bDROP\\b"),"DROP",
    match(_raw,"(?i)\\bREJECT\\b"),"REJECT",
    1=1,verdict)
| eval src_ip=trim(coalesce(src_ip,src_addr,cip,src,""))
| eval dst_ip=trim(coalesce(dst_ip,dst_addr,dip,dest_ip,""))
| eval rule_tag_raw=trim(coalesce(rule_tag,RULE_TAG,ruleTag,rule_tag_string,""))
| rex field=rule_tag_raw "^(?<seq_hint>[^|]+)\\|(?<policy_hint>[^|]+)\\|(?<rule_numeric>[^|]+)$"
| eval proto=upper(trim(coalesce(protocol,proto,ip_proto,"TCP")))
| eval vnic_mark=trim(coalesce(vnic_port_id,dvport_id,port_key,lp_port_id,""))
| eval idfw_user=trim(coalesce(USERNAME,user_name,idfw_principal,sso_username,""))
| rex field=_raw "(?i)INET\\s+match\\s+(?<pkt_inet_action>ALLOW|DROP|REJECT)"
| lookup nsx_dfw_group_membership ip AS src_ip OUTPUT group_display_name AS src_group, resource_path AS src_group_path
| lookup nsx_dfw_group_membership ip AS dst_ip OUTPUT group_display_name AS dst_group, resource_path AS dst_group_path
| eval enforcement_finding=case(
    (isnull(src_group) OR src_group="") AND verdict_norm IN ("ALLOW","REJECT"),"unmapped_source_on_permit",
    (isnull(dst_group) OR dst_group="") AND verdict_norm="ALLOW","allow_toward_peer_outside_nsgroups",
    verdict_norm="ALLOW" AND len(src_group)>0 AND len(dst_group)>0 AND src_group_path!=dst_group_path,"cross_nsg_allow_needs_review",
    verdict_norm IN ("DROP","REJECT") AND src_group==dst_group AND len(src_group)>0,"same_nsg_deny_signal",
    1=1,"baseline_flow")
| bin _time span=1h
| stats count AS flow_events,
        dc(dst_ip) AS dst_card,
        dc(src_ip) AS src_card,
        sum(eval(if(verdict_norm=="ALLOW",1,0))) AS allow_cnt,
        sum(eval(if(verdict_norm IN ("DROP","REJECT"),1,0))) AS deny_cnt,
        values(policy_hint) AS policy_names,
        values(rule_numeric) AS rule_ids,
        latest(idfw_user) AS last_user_ctx,
        values(vnic_mark) AS vnic_samples,
        values(proto) AS protos,
        latest(pkt_inet_action) AS inet_sig
    BY _time enforcement_finding src_group dst_group verdict_norm
| eventstats sum(flow_events) AS hour_total BY _time
| eval mix_pct=round(100*flow_events/nullif(hour_total,0),2)
| sort - flow_events
| table _time enforcement_finding src_group dst_group verdict_norm flow_events allow_cnt deny_cnt dst_card src_card mix_pct policy_names rule_ids last_user_ctx vnic_samples protos inet_sig
description: Audits whether VMware **NSX** **Distributed Firewall** enforcement still matches the documented micro-segmentation design by correlating **`vmware:nsxt:dfw_groups`** membership snapshots and **`vmware:nsxt:dfw_policy`** **`applied_to`** / **`category`** sequencing against live **`vmware:nsxt:firewall`** verdicts enriched through **`nsx_dfw_group_membership`** lookups: it surfaces **NSGroup** coverage gaps (**workloads whose IPs never resolve to a modeled group**), **cross-NSGroup** **ALLOW** pairs that deserve **shadow-rule** review, **intra-NSGroup** **DROP** signals that contradict **tier-to-tier** diagrams, and hours when posture collectors stop so teams do not mistake missing data for a clean bill of health—distinct from pure traffic trending because it asks whether the **blueprint** still wraps the right **VMs**, not merely how loud the **ALLOW** counters became.
value: Segregation blueprints rot quietly when **tags** slip during cloning, **DR** failovers park guests on new segments, or **imports** widen **`applied_to`** scopes while **`Application`** sections stay unchanged on paper. Giving **GRC**, **platform**, and **incident response** joint evidence that **intent** (**Policy API** rows) and **enforcement** (**vsip** verdict + **NSGroup** resolution) stayed aligned collapses multi-week audit scrambles, prevents **‘open bar’ east-west** periods after sloppy **DEFAULT** edits, and funds targeted engineering hours on real drift rather than chasing **UC-18.2.1** volume noise that merely reflects bursty batch jobs.
implementation: Ingest **`vmware:nsxt:dfw_groups`** and **`vmware:nsxt:dfw_policy`** via scheduled **HTTPS** collectors mirroring **NSX Policy** **GET** endpoints; maintain Splunk lookups expanding **NSGroup** membership per IP; run the **`spl`** saved search hourly; route **`enforcement_finding`** tiers to **IAM**, **network security**, and **app** owners; validate against **NSX Manager** UI exports weekly.
visualization: (1) **Column** chart of **`flow_events`** by **`enforcement_finding`**. (2) **Sankey** of **`src_group_path`→`dst_group_path`** for **`cross_nsg_allow_needs_review`**. (3) **Heatmap** of **`mix_pct`**. (4) **Table** with **`rule_ids`**, **`policy_names`**, **`inet_sig`**. (5) **Single value** of **`unmapped_source_on_permit`** distinct counts.
schema: CIM: Network_Traffic for **DFW** syslog tuples; Change for **Policy** / **NSGroup** JSON revisions mirrored into Splunk.
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate **Network_Traffic** once **`FIELDALIAS`** maps **`src_ip`**, **`dst_ip`**, **`action`**, **`transport`**, and **`rule`** identifiers from **`vmware:nsxt:firewall`**; use **Change** acceleration sparingly on **`object`** / **`object_id`** fields extracted from **`vmware:nsxt:dfw_policy`** only if summaries remain bounded.

references.title: vDefend Firewall with Advanced Threat Prevention overview (NSX administration guide)

knownFalsePositives: **tag_sync_lag_after_clone** — Fresh vSphere templates inherit stale custom attributes for NSGroup Tag expressions; vmware:nsxt:firewall ALLOW verdicts appear while Splunk lookups still list the guest as uncovered until the next dfw_groups poll—suppress short windows anchored to clone task IDs. **ad_group_eval_refresh_cycle** — Active Directory–backed NSGroups pause membership during ldap failovers; allow_toward_peer_outside_nsgroups spikes even though the manager already evaluates the true subset—cross-check Identity service alarms before paging. **snapshot_poll_skew_hour_boundary** — Hourly bin boundaries split a single burst across buckets, dimming mix_pct—use align=@s offsets when forensic precision matters. **staging_segment_overlap_noise** — Pre-production VLAN overlays deliberately share address space with production in some labs; cross_nsg_allow_needs_review flags benign pairs—separate indexes or environment tags to silence labs. **intelligence_recommendation_publish_queue** — NSX Intelligence backlog delays suggested DROP commits while temporary ALLOW persists; combine with the Intelligence UI queue depth before blaming drift. **ephemeral_ip_nat_pool_churn** — Edge SNAT pools or Kubernetes NodePort layers reuse public-looking addresses absent from NSGroup expressions—expect false orphan classifications unless internal ranges feed the lookup. **cross_vc_universal_group_replication_lag** — Federation sites may replicate groups minutes behind policy objects; resource_path mismatches self-heal—extend alert suppression with site_id metadata instead of deleting detections. **dfw_logging_mode_surge** — Operators enable verbose connection logging briefly, flooding flow_events without changing posture—correlate with change tickets that reference logging level knobs. **static_member_export_truncation** — Very large NSGroup static member lists occasionally paginate across API calls; if collectors ingest only the first page, lookups miss late-alphabet hosts until the next full export—watch collector byte caps and HTTP 206 handling.
detailedImplementation:
**Prerequisites**
• **Splunk TA footprint** — Install `Splunk_TA_vmware-nsxt` ([Splunkbase 4856](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4856)) on Heavy Forwarders or search heads that parse `vmware:nsxt:firewall` / `vmware:nsxt:idfw` for VMware NSX **4.2**, supported **4.1** trains, or maintenance **NSX-T 3.2** fabrics per Broadcom lifecycle statements.
• **REST posture collectors** — Schedule HTTPS jobs (Python modular input, **AWS Lambda** collector, or **`cron` + `curl`**) authenticated with `POST /api/session/create` or OAuth-backed service accounts to pull `GET /policy/api/v1/infra/domains/default/groups`, `GET /policy/api/v1/infra/domains/default/security-policies`, and nested `.../security-policies/{policy-id}/rules` payloads into `vmware:nsxt:dfw_groups` and `vmware:nsxt:dfw_policy` sourcetypes with stable keys such as `resource_path`, `id`, `expression`, `applied_to`, `category`, `sequence_number`, `source_groups`, `destination_groups`, and `action` so Splunk compares intent to runtime logs offline.
• **Inventory joins** — Deploy `Splunk_TA_vmware` ([Splunkbase 1810](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1810)) so `vmware:vmware:vsphere:vcenter` guest IPv4/IPv6 fields enrich bare addresses emitted inside DFW syslog lines when dynamic NSGroup criteria (tags, segment, AD group) lag a few polls behind vMotion events.
• **Manager alarms** — Keep `vmware:nsxt:syslog` streaming `NSX Manager` health events so missed API polls that silently freeze group membership exports correlate with syslog silence instead of false “all clear” panels.
• **Lookup authoring** — Build `transforms.conf` definitions like `nsx_dfw_group_membership` that expand nightly `vmware:nsxt:dfw_groups` JSON into one row per workload IP ↔ `group_display_name` ↔ `resource_path`; refresh via `outputlookup` after each poll.
• RBAC — Collector credentials require read access to **Policy** objects; never embed personal `admin` cookies—use least-privilege **audit** personas with `read` on `infra` paths only.
• **Licensing math** — `vmware:nsxt:firewall` volume still dominates license; policy snapshots add a small index tail yet prevent expensive UI scraping during investigations.

**Step 1 — Configure data collection**
(1) **Syslog path** — Forward `dfwpktlogs.log` fingerprints from ESXi **vsip** modules exactly as **UC-18.2.1** documents; keep `RULE_TAG` tuples intact because they stitch verdict lines back to `security-policies` rows.
(2) **Group snapshots** — Every poll should emit one JSON event per `/infra/domains/default/groups/{uuid}` with hashed `expression` text plus expanded static members when the API returns them; store `_time` at ingestion receipt, not backdated NSX revision timestamps, so Splunk `latest` semantics reflect collector truth.
(3) **Policy snapshots** — Capture each rule’s `source_groups`, `destination_groups`, `services`, `profiles`, `logged`, **`scope` / `applied_to`**, and `category` (Emergency, Infrastructure, Environment, Application, Default) so later searches spot Default-section drift that accidentally widens `ANY` reach.
(4) **IDFW split** — When `USERNAME` parsing requires different `LINE_BREAKER` settings, route enriched lines to `vmware:nsxt:idfw` but keep `FIELDALIAS` chains aligned so `src_ip` / `dst_ip` names match the firewall sourcetype.
(5) **SC4S option** — For fleets beyond **10k EPS**, land syslog on **Splunk Connect for Syslog** clusters with **RFC 5424** framing preserved; verify `RULE_TAG` never truncates at relay boundaries.

**Step 2 — Author macros and embed SPL**
Promote reusable `eval` blocks into `macros/nsx_enforcement_base` so multiple saved searches (orphan coverage, cross-group ALLOW review, intra-group DROP hunts) share the same normalization logic. Version-control the macro beside your governed app.

```spl
`comment("UC-18.2.2 VMware NSX micro-segmentation enforcement posture — NSGroup coverage vs DFW verdicts. Tunables earliest=-24h@h span=1h")`
(index=vmware OR index=vmware_nsx OR index=network_sec) earliest=-24h@h latest=now (sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:firewall" OR sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:idfw")
| eval verdict=upper(trim(coalesce(dfw_action,action,firewall_action,match_action,"")))
| eval verdict_norm=case(
    match(verdict,"^(ALLOW|ACCEPT)$"),"ALLOW",
    match(verdict,"^(DROP|DENY)$"),"DROP",
    match(verdict,"^(REJECT|RESET)$"),"REJECT",
    match(_raw,"(?i)\\bALLOW\\b"),"ALLOW",
    match(_raw,"(?i)\\bDROP\\b"),"DROP",
    match(_raw,"(?i)\\bREJECT\\b"),"REJECT",
    1=1,verdict)
| eval src_ip=trim(coalesce(src_ip,src_addr,cip,src,""))
| eval dst_ip=trim(coalesce(dst_ip,dst_addr,dip,dest_ip,""))
| eval rule_tag_raw=trim(coalesce(rule_tag,RULE_TAG,ruleTag,rule_tag_string,""))
| rex field=rule_tag_raw "^(?<seq_hint>[^|]+)\\|(?<policy_hint>[^|]+)\\|(?<rule_numeric>[^|]+)$"
| eval proto=upper(trim(coalesce(protocol,proto,ip_proto,"TCP")))
| eval vnic_mark=trim(coalesce(vnic_port_id,dvport_id,port_key,lp_port_id,""))
| eval idfw_user=trim(coalesce(USERNAME,user_name,idfw_principal,sso_username,""))
| rex field=_raw "(?i)INET\\s+match\\s+(?<pkt_inet_action>ALLOW|DROP|REJECT)"
| lookup nsx_dfw_group_membership ip AS src_ip OUTPUT group_display_name AS src_group, resource_path AS src_group_path
| lookup nsx_dfw_group_membership ip AS dst_ip OUTPUT group_display_name AS dst_group, resource_path AS dst_group_path
| eval enforcement_finding=case(
    (isnull(src_group) OR src_group="") AND verdict_norm IN ("ALLOW","REJECT"),"unmapped_source_on_permit",
    (isnull(dst_group) OR dst_group="") AND verdict_norm="ALLOW","allow_toward_peer_outside_nsgroups",
    verdict_norm="ALLOW" AND len(src_group)>0 AND len(dst_group)>0 AND src_group_path!=dst_group_path,"cross_nsg_allow_needs_review",
    verdict_norm IN ("DROP","REJECT") AND src_group==dst_group AND len(src_group)>0,"same_nsg_deny_signal",
    1=1,"baseline_flow")
| bin _time span=1h
| stats count AS flow_events,
        dc(dst_ip) AS dst_card,
        dc(src_ip) AS src_card,
        sum(eval(if(verdict_norm=="ALLOW",1,0))) AS allow_cnt,
        sum(eval(if(verdict_norm IN ("DROP","REJECT"),1,0))) AS deny_cnt,
        values(policy_hint) AS policy_names,
        values(rule_numeric) AS rule_ids,
        latest(idfw_user) AS last_user_ctx,
        values(vnic_mark) AS vnic_samples,
        values(proto) AS protos,
        latest(pkt_inet_action) AS inet_sig
    BY _time enforcement_finding src_group dst_group verdict_norm
| eventstats sum(flow_events) AS hour_total BY _time
| eval mix_pct=round(100*flow_events/nullif(hour_total,0),2)
| sort - flow_events
| table _time enforcement_finding src_group dst_group verdict_norm flow_events allow_cnt deny_cnt dst_card src_card mix_pct policy_names rule_ids last_user_ctx vnic_samples protos inet_sig
```

**Search logic notes**
• `verdict_norm` aligns ALLOW / DROP / REJECT verbs whether they arrive in extracted fields or raw `INET match` narratives—parser upgrades across NSX trains therefore do not fracture charts.
• `lookup nsx_dfw_group_membership` turns IP literals into `resource_path` pointers; when the lookup misses, the `unmapped_source_on_permit` and `allow_toward_peer_outside_nsgroups` branches surface coverage gaps—the workload sits outside modeled NSGroups or the lookup refresh stalled.
• `cross_nsg_allow_needs_review` highlights east-west ALLOW verdicts where source and destination resolved to different `resource_path` values; pair with `vmware:nsxt:dfw_policy` to see if a `sequence_number` above a DROP rule should have fired instead (**shadowed rule** investigation).
• `same_nsg_deny_signal` calls attention to DROP/REJECT inside one NSGroup label—often operator error on **Intra** destined services or a missing ALLOW pin between **tiers** that the design doc promised.
• `mix_pct` contextualizes how much of each hour a given `enforcement_finding` consumes—useful when prioritizing noisy **shared services** pairs versus rare **orphan** IPs.

**Step 3 — Validate**
(1) **`NSX Manager` Policy UI** — Open **Networking → Connectivity → Segments** then **Security → Distributed Firewall**; pick a `RULE_TAG` from Splunk and confirm `policy_hint` / `rule_numeric` match the `security-policies` editor for the same **domain**.
(2) **Group membership** — Compare `lookup` output for a suspect IP against **Inventory → Groups** detail panes; mismatches usually mean `expression` used **Tag** criteria that **vCenter** shows as stale, not a Splunk parsing bug.
(3) **`applied_to` scope** — When Splunk shows surging `cross_nsg_allow_needs_review`, verify whether the live policy `scope` still limits rules to intended NSGroups or drifted to `DFW`-wide application after an import.
(4) **Traceflow** — Use **Plan Tier-1** (or supported **Traceflow** entry points) to replay a sampled ALLOW tuple and confirm **Layer-4/7** services match the Splunk row—especially when **Context Profiles** gate ALLOW decisions.
(5) **Alarm correlation** — If `vmware:nsxt:syslog` reports Corfu or **cluster** health issues during blank `dfw_groups` hours, treat missing posture data as **unknown**, not **healthy**.

**Step 4 — Operationalise**
(1) **Owner map** — Route `unmapped_source_on_permit` tickets to platform engineering, `cross_nsg_allow_needs_review` to `security-policies` stewards, and `same_nsg_deny_signal` to application teams with architecture diagrams in hand.
(2) **Evidence vault** — Snap **`GET /policy/api/v1/infra/domains/default/security-policies` exports beside Splunk `outputlookup` CSV blobs weekly so auditors see matching hashes between **intent** and **indexed** posture.
(3) **Suppression hygiene** — Tie documented maintenance windows to `src_group` labels when large fleets intentionally move subnets; avoid blanketing suppression on entire indexes.
(4) **Lookup SLA** — Alert when `nsx_dfw_group_membership` row counts fall by **>30%** hour-over-hour—the symptom precedes bogus orphan classifications.
(5) **Runbook links** — Embed deep links to **vDefend** Distributed Firewall chapters inside `custom_setup` fields so on-call engineers land on vendor context fast.

**Step 5 — Visualize, alert, and troubleshoot**
**Visualization** — (1) **Stacked time-chart** of `flow_events` by `enforcement_finding` split `verdict_norm` so posture drift dominates timelines instead of raw EPS. (2) **Sankey** from `src_group_path` → `dst_group_path` filtered to `cross_nsg_allow_needs_review` for leadership-ready isolation storytelling. (3) **Heatmap** of `mix_pct` × `hour_total` to expose slow-burn leaks. (4) **Single-value** panels for distinct `unmapped_source_on_permit` cardinality. (5) **Table** with `inet_sig`, `protos`, `rule_ids`, and `last_user_ctx` for **Identity Firewall** rehearsals.

**Alert design** — Page when `unmapped_source_on_permit` `flow_events` exceeds `50` in `15m` **and** `hour_total` `>2000` (tune per tenant). Warn when `cross_nsg_allow_needs_review` `allow_cnt` surpasses a rolling **7-day** baseline by `3σ`. Create **KV-store** correlation to open IR tickets if `same_nsg_deny_signal` spikes simultaneously with malware AV positives on the same `src_group`. Send daily CSV exports of `allow_toward_peer_outside_nsgroups` to GRC teams for **segregation** attestations. Throttle duplicates with `throttle` fields keyed on `src_group`, `dst_group`, `policy_names`.

**Troubleshooting**
• **Blank `lookup` columns** — `vmware:nsxt:dfw_groups` polls failed; check collector HTTP logs for **401/403** or TLS interception—not **props.conf** first.
• **Exploding `values()` MVs** — Cap with `mvfilter` or move to `sparkline`-friendly **posture summaries** when `rule_ids` exceed practical UI limits.
• **`baseline_flow` dominance** — Not a failure; pivot to `rare` command weekly to surface slow ALLOW creep.
• **IPv6 literal mismatches** — Normalize **compressed** versus **expanded** forms upstream before **lookup** keys diverge.
• **Duplicate Manager clusters** — `site_id` metadata must disambiguate identically named NSGroups across **federation** sites.
• **Stale `applied_to` in JSON** — API may cache; shorten poll cadence during **blue/green** policy pushes.
• **Too many `cross_nsg_allow` rows** — Confirm whether `shared_services` NSGroup legitimately fronts the traffic before opening tickets.
• **Identity blanks** — `idfw_user` nulls during AD outages should not mute segmentation evidence—split dashboards.
• **EPS hot spots** — Heavy **Connection** logging modes swamp posture searches—coordinate observability knobs with **UC-18.2.1** owners.
• **Lookup rotation locks** — Windows or NFS locks on `nsx_dfw_group_membership.csv` stall refresh; monitor `lookup` failure messages in `splunkd.log`.
grandmaExplanation: We verify that each guest computer still sits in the right security bucket, that firewall blueprints still wrap the workloads they should, and that pairs of teams only talk when the design says they may.
hardware: VMware NSX **Distributed Firewall** enforcement runs in the **ESXi** hypervisor **vsip** chain on supported rack servers (Dell **PowerEdge R650/R750**, HPE **ProLiant DL360/DL380**, Cisco **UCS C-Series**, Lenovo **ThinkSystem SR650**) with **NSX Manager** clusters hosting the **Policy** API. This UC assumes kernel-mode **DFW** on **vSphere** transport nodes—not **Gateway Firewall** **VM** form factors on **Edge** bare metal.
